name: nasa-jpl
tagline: If a rule can't be checked by a machine, it's a suggestion. Ship only what analyzers, assertions, and reviewers have all cleared.
philosophy: Safety-critical discipline: zero warnings, bounded everything, an explicit written disposition for every finding. There is no 'minor' tier.

gates.description: Compile/typecheck with maximum pedantry; a single warning in changed code blocks GREEN.
EVERY reviewer finding gets an explicit builder response — fixed, or a written disagree-justification surfaced to the human. Nothing is silently dropped.
No unbounded loops, unbounded recursion, or post-init dynamic allocation in safety-flagged paths; all return values checked.
Functions beyond ~60 lines in changed code are findings requiring justification.
Touched functions should assert their pre/postconditions and invariants (target: two per function in C-like code); the reviewer counts them.
GREEN requires zero undispositioned findings. There is no merging with open findings.
